opportunities.rationale: NVDA Q1 FY27 reports May 20 AMC with consensus revenue ~$78-80B (+78% YoY) and EPS ~$1.77; Citi sees $1.4B upside beat. Polymarket assigns ~90% beat probability. RSI likely elevated but momentum confirms NVDA has beaten 4/4 last quarters. Invalidated if guidance disappointed or revenue misses $76B floor.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter before close May 20 on dip to $128-132 range; risk-off if VIX spikes above 20 post-FOMC minutes.

opportunities.rationale: WMT Q1 FY27 reports May 21 BMO with implied move of 4.98% (options market). Consumer Defensive sector is the #2 weekly performer (+3.14% WTD). Walmart is a defensive bellwether with strong cost discipline and grocery market-share gains. Invalidated if management issues cautious guidance citing tariff-driven consumer softness similar to Q4 cautionary tone.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter May 20 close on confirmation that NVDA sentiment is positive; re-evaluate if FOMC minutes are hawkish.

opportunities.rationale: TJX Q1 FY27 reports May 20. Off-price retail benefits from trade-down consumer behaviour as tariff uncertainty pressures discretionary spending. Sector rotation into defensive consumer names supports setup. Beat 3+ quarters recently. Invalidated if gross margin compresses beyond guidance due to sourcing cost increases.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter May 19-20 in $127-130 zone; exit after earnings if gap-up exceeds 5%.

opportunities.rationale: LOW Q1 FY27 reports May 21. Home improvement a key consumer bellwether with housing starts and building permits also due May 21. HD reported today (EPS $3.43 beat $3.41 est, rev $41.8B beat) but fell -2.49% on margin concerns — LOW may follow lower in sympathy unless it guides better. Invalidated below $228 if housing data disappoints materially.
opportunities.trade_setup: home improvement pair with HD watch
opportunities.entry_trigger: Monitor HD post-market reaction for coattail signal; enter LOW if HD rallies in AH or gap closes.

opportunities.rationale: INTU Q3 FY26 reports ~May 22. Tax season tailwinds and accelerating AI-powered TurboTax/QuickBooks adoption. Software stocks benefiting from trending bullish regime with S&P above all MAs. Analyst consensus remains Overweight with PTs implying 15%+ upside. Invalidated below $665 if AI monetisation disappoints or enterprise spending slows.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter May 20-21 if market holds gains post-NVDA; avoid day of earnings if VIX exceeds 20.

opportunities.rationale: TGT Q1 FY27 reports May 21. Consumer discretionary laggard (-2.65% WTD) which lowers the bar for a positive surprise. Defensive rotation underway; TGT could surprise if grocery/essentials mix improves margins. LOW conviction given Consumer Cyclical sector headwinds and prior guidance cuts. Invalidated below $96 crash-low if consumer spending weakens.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Only enter if WMT gaps up positively May 21 — coattail into TGT same day.
